news.headline: US to sell $85 bln 4-week bills on April 10th, to settle on April 15th.

news.summary: According to recent reports, the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.8%, the lowest it has been in over a decade. This decline in unemployment has led to the creation of 250,000 new jobs in the past month alone, with the manufacturing and healthcare sectors experiencing the most growth. Additionally, wages have seen a 2.5% increase, providing some relief to workers. The positive trends in the job market have boosted consumer confidence, leading to increased spending and economic growth. The government is optimistic about the future outlook as they continue to focus on policies that support job creation and economic development.
news.headline: FDIC plans to relax banks’ living-will requirements, says acting chair. #finance #news

news.summary: Enphase Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) stocks have dipped as the company announced the release of its latest battery technology in Luxembourg. The IQ Battery 5P with FlexPhase is the most powerful and versatile battery offered by the company, catering to three-phase electrical setups commonly found in Luxembourg residences, providing reliable backup power. This new product, which initially offers 5 kWh capacity, follows its successful launch in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. The system can be configured with multiple units for higher capacity.
news.headline: Enphase Energy introduces IQ Battery 5P in Luxembourg, offering 15-year warranty. Supports 3-phase electrical systems and starts at 5 kWh capacity.

news.summary: Executives from cryptocurrency firms Messari and Sygnum are optimistic about institutional Bitcoin adoption in the latter half of 2025 despite the market turmoil caused by US trade tariffs. They predict a significant shift in the banking sector's involvement with crypto in the near future. Messari CEO Eric Turner and Sygnum Bank co-founder Thomas Eichenberger expect global banking interest in Bitcoin services to increase as regulators embrace cryptocurrencies. This push comes as regulatory bodies, including the SEC, show more openness towards crypto. Eichenberger also suggests that international banks with US branches are preparing to enter the market once the regulatory landscape becomes clearer. With clear crypto rules for banks, large international banks are expected to offer crypto-related services without fear of regulatory backlash. This panel discussion at Paris Blockchain Week highlighted the growing positive sentiment towards crypto adoption and regulatory acceptance within the banking sector.
news.headline: Crypto industry execs anticipate global banking to embrace Bitcoin by end of 2025, as regulators welcome crypto. Excitement for Q3 and Q4.

news.summary: In a joint announcement made by AMTD Group and The Generation Essentials Group, it has been revealed that Julia Michalska has been appointed as the Global Editor-in-Chief and Benjamin Sutton as the Editor-in-Chief, Americas, of The Art Newspaper. Founded in 1990, The Art Newspaper is a global art media company. Michalska and Sutton will join a global editorial committee responsible for overseeing the content and maintaining the quality of The Art Newspaper worldwide. The company, in existence for 35 years, is set to benefit from the new leadership appointments.
news.headline: AMTD Group appoints Julia Michalska as Global Editor-in-Chief & Benjamin Sutton as Editor-in-Chief, Americas for The Art Newspaper.

news.summary: Monaco displayed its increasing involvement in space exploration through various events that included prominent figures in the industry.
news.headline: Monaco demonstrates key role in space exploration with signature events, highlighting leaders in industry.

news.summary: Global stock markets rebounded following a decline triggered by President Trump's tariffs, with China introducing fresh initiatives to boost its markets. This led to a recovery in stock prices worldwide after they had initially experienced a drop due to the tariffs imposed by President Trump. The measures implemented by China contributed to the improvement in market conditions, helping to offset some of the losses incurred earlier. Investors responded positively to these developments, leading to an overall recovery in stock values across different countries.
news.headline: Global stocks bounce back after President Trump's tariffs, as China unveils measures to boost markets.

news.summary: ZKcandy, a product of iCandy Interactive and Matter Labs, is an Ethereum layer-2 network that specifically targets mobile gaming.
news.headline: New Ethereum Gaming Network ZKcandy launched, including 'Pepe Kingdom.' Built by iCandy Interactive & Matter Labs, it targets mobile gaming.

news.summary: Following three consecutive days of significant selling, the major stock market indexes saw an increase on Tuesday morning. Both Nvidia and Palantir experienced a rebound in their stock prices. The Dow Jones surged by 1,200 points, marking a strong recovery after the recent downturn. Additionally, health insurers witnessed a notable rise in their stock values during this period. The positive movement in the market was highlighted in live coverage by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Dow Jones rises by 1,200 points after 3-day drop; Health insurers soar, Nvidia & Palantir rebound.

news.summary: The Management Board of "UTENOS TRIKOTAŽAS," a restructured joint stock company with the company code 183709468 and a total of 9,503,000 shares, has scheduled the Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders to take place on April 30, 2025, at 11:00 am at the company's registered office in Utena. Shareholders can register from 10:00 am to 10:55 am on the same day, with the record date set as April 23, 2025. Additionally, the number of shares with voting rights is also 9,503,000, and the record date for shareholders' rights is May 15, 2025.
news.headline: Notice of Ordinary General Meeting for restructuring AB Utenos trikotažas on April 30, 2025, with 9,503,000 total shares/voting rights. Registration from 10:00-10:55am, record dates April 23 and May 15, 2025.

news.summary: On April 7, 2025, Better Choice Company, Inc. (NYSE American: BTTR), a pet health and wellness firm based in Tampa, Florida, disclosed that its Board of Directors had sanctioned a stock dividend of 0.35 per share for each share of common stock held. This dividend will be distributed to shareholders listed as of Thursday, April 17, 2025.
news.headline: Better Choice Company delays payment date for stock dividend to April 17, 2025, offering 0.35 per share to every 1 share held.
US Trade Representative Greer: Trump has been clear that he won't do tariff exemptions in the near term.

news.summary: According to a statement made by Jamieson Greer, the trade representative of the United States, the American economy requires urgent and long-awaited transformation.
news.headline: Trump officials attempt to reassure markets amid tariff concerns. U.S. trade rep emphasizes urgent need for economic reform in senate meeting.

news.summary: Seertech Solutions, a prominent player in learning enablement, is enhancing its customer-focused innovation through recent executive appointments and expanded capabilities. The company has recorded significant successes in various industries, reinforcing its position as a global leader in the field. With a series of major customer wins, Seertech is capitalizing on its growth momentum. The developments were announced on April 8, 2025, in Denver.
news.headline: Seertech thrives with new executive hires, expanded capabilities, major customer wins, and global growth.

news.summary: Cosmos Health has introduced Sky Premium Life to Albania in partnership with distributor Pharma Cell. The company has secured an initial annual order worth $300,000.
news.headline: Cosmos Health partners with Pharma Cell to launch Sky Premium Life in Albania, secures $300,000 initial order.

news.summary: RPM International Inc. reported a 3% decline in sales to $1.48 billion in the fiscal third quarter, missing the consensus of $1.51 billion. Sales were impacted by weak demand in specialty OEM manufacturing markets and unfavorable weather conditions. Adjusted EPS was $0.35, lower than the expected $0.49, and adjusted EBIT fell 29% to $78.2 million due to various factors. Operating cash flow for the nine months ended February 28 was $619.0 million, with total debt decreasing to $2.10 billion. Capital expenditures rose to $158.9 million. Shares of RPM International Inc. are trading lower following the earnings report.
news.headline: RPM Q3: Sales decline 3% to $1.48 billion, missing consensus of $1.51 billion. Adjusted EPS $0.35 vs $0.49 consensus. Adjusted EBIT falls 29% to $78.2 million due to various factors. Operating cash flow $619.0 million, total debt decreased to $2.10 billion. Capital expenditures rose to $158.9 million. More details on Benzinga.com.
US Trade Representative Greer: We are trying to limit imports driven by unfair trade practices while promoting US exports.
Mexico's President Sheinbaum: The majority of directors of auto plants in Mexico said that as of now, they have no plans to move their factories.

news.summary: A prominent travel marketplace and AI technology company, Mondee, has recently been acquired and recapitalized. This development marks a significant move in the industry, with Mondee being recognized for its innovative approach and success. The acquisition and recapitalization of Mondee emphasize the growing importance and value of AI technology within the travel sector. This strategic decision is expected to bring about positive changes and improvements in the company's operations and services. The specifics of the deal, including the exact figures involved, have not been disclosed publicly as of now.
news.headline: Tabhi acquires Mondee post Chapter 11, enhancing liquidity and capital structure for the leading AI travel company.

news.summary: On April 4, 2025, SIMPPLE Ltd. (NASDAQ: SPPL), a prominent technology company in the facilities management industry, disclosed that they secured a new contract worth $524,000. This agreement pertains to the provision of autonomous cleaning robotics at Singapore's international airport.
news.headline: SIMPPLE Ltd. secures $524,000 contract to provide cleaning robots at Singapore Airport; 2nd deal.

news.summary: Market attention is currently focused on President Donald Trump's trade wars, as stocks took a significant hit on Thursday amid concerns that his latest tariff policies could hinder economic growth.
news.headline: Trump's trade wars impacting economy, stocks plummeted over fears. High stakes in new jobs report.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures dropped on Friday after a significant fall on Thursday due to President Trump's tariffs. The Dow Jones saw a massive drop of 1,679 points, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also experienced their largest single-day losses since June and March 2020. Trump remained optimistic about the market's performance. Bond yields were at 3.94% for the 10-year Treasury and 3.61% for the two-year bond. The market predicts a 69.4% chance of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates. The futures changes were: Dow Jones -1.00%, S&P 500 -0.78%, Nasdaq 100 -0.54%, and Russell 2000 -1.42%. ETFs like SPY and QQQ also declined. Technology stocks, including Apple, Nvidia, and Amazon, faced significant losses; however, consumer staples stocks thrived, with Lamb Weston Holdings Inc. experiencing a 10% surge after exceeding financial expectations. Economic data showed a decrease in initial jobless claims and an improvement in the U.S. trade deficit, but the ISM services PMI declined in March.
news.headline: Stocks continue to plummet after Trump's tariff announcement, with Dow dropping by 1,679 points and S&P 500 and Nasdaq facing their biggest losses since 2020. Trump optimistic, but futures show decline in major indices. Treasury bonds at 3.94% (10-year) and 3.61% (2-year). Markets predict 69.4% likelihood of unchanged interest rates by May. Notable declines in tech sector stocks. Apple down 9.3%, Nvidia down 7.8%, Amazon down 8.98%. Consumer staples sector stands strong, with Lamb Weston Holdings Inc. seeing a 10% surge after exceeding financial expectations. Mixed economic data with lower jobless claims but declining PMI.

news.summary: CEO Peter Mallouk of Creative Planning has outlined three potential economic scenarios in light of the escalating global trade war caused by U.S. tariffs. The worst-case scenario involves a long-term U.S. tariff policy leading to consumer price spikes, inflation, and a 30-60% stock market plunge, plunging the country into a deep recession. Alternatively, a best-case scenario envisions successful negotiations within six months resulting in lower interest rates and benefits for U.S. consumers and businesses. A third "mostly bad scenario" implies that delays in negotiations could still have negative impacts. Mallouk warns of the risks associated with the current trade policy shift, emphasizing the importance of strategic trade deals for economic stability.
news.headline: Analyst warns of 60% market crash risk amid worst tariff scenario, predicts recession & soaring consumer prices.

news.summary: Over the weekend, ticket sales reached an impressive $163 million according to Warner Bros., exceeding initial expectations and marking a significant turnaround for the Hollywood industry.
news.headline: Surprise hit 'Minecraft Movie' rakes in $163M over the weekend, surpassing expectations and boosting box office numbers.

news.summary: Bitcoin surged back to $80,000 on Tuesday, sparking optimism among traders for a potential breakout week ahead. A crypto trader, Astronomer, executed a well-timed BTC long position as the local low was established. This move was not a result of hindsight but due to various bullish signals such as a stretched CME gap, a weekly open above, a fakeout downswing on Monday, smart money accumulation during retail selling, and an overall positive market sentiment that had been developing for weeks. Despite Bitcoin creating a lower low, the successful reclaim of $80,000 confirmed the bullish scenario. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin surged to $80,000 triggering optimism among traders for a potential breakout week. Key signals suggested a bullish setup with a well-timed long trade based on stretched CME gap, smart money accumulation, and positive macro bias.

news.summary: The positive impact of free trade on numerous countries worldwide has been so significant that it is conceivable for the global community to manage without its largest participant.
news.headline: Trump's tariffs may harm free trade, but world may adapt without top player. Free trade's benefits to many countries can endure.

news.summary: Stock market futures experienced a significant decline after China’s retaliation against Trump’s tariffs, following the market's most substantial losses since 2020. The focus is now on the upcoming jobs report and statements from Powell. This caused a downturn in Dow Jones futures amid concerns of a potential recession.
news.headline: Dow Jones futures plummet as China retaliates against Trump tariffs, sparking recession fears post worst losses since 2020.

news.summary: An innovative vehicle produced in the United States developed in partnership with Roush and Chang Robotics is set to be launched in Jacksonville, Florida on April 8, 2025. The vehicle, created by Kodiak Technologies, introduces a unique power feature allowing for mobile power export. This cutting-edge development signifies a significant advancement in the industry, showcasing dual-use capability.
news.headline: Kodiak Technologies to reveal powerful electric snow removal vehicle at 2025 Snow Symposium with dual-use power capability. Made in the USA.

news.summary: As a result of the recent controversy surrounding the CEO of Tesla, vehicles from the company that have been sold or traded in are now available at discounted prices on various sales platforms. This backlash has led to a significant decrease in the prices of these Teslas, making them attractive deals for interested buyers.
news.headline: Tesla resale demand rises amid backlash against CEO. Owners selling cars at discounts, flooding resale market.

news.summary: Senate Republicans are aiming to exclude Democrats from influencing the future of President Trump’s tax cuts from 2017, a strategy that could potentially backfire as Democrats might leverage this in retaliation.
news.headline: GOP aims to secure permanent status for Trump's 2017 tax cuts. Senate GOP looks to prevent Dem intervention in the decision.

news.summary: The President has promised to support entrepreneurs by lowering taxes and cutting bureaucratic hurdles. However, some business owners claim that certain regulations have left them in a less favorable position.
news.headline: Small businesses are grappling with a myriad of challenges including cuts, freezes, and tariffs. Despite promises of tax reductions and regulatory easing by President Trump, some owners feel disadvantaged.

news.summary: Today, the Dow Jones saw a sharp decline of 1,100 points due to escalating fears surrounding the U.S.-China trade war. This resulted in significant drops in the stock prices of tech giants Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla. The market turmoil painted a bleak picture for investors and analysts.
news.headline: Dow Jones drops 1,100 points due to US-China trade war fears; Apple, Nvidia, Tesla stocks plummet on market.

news.summary: Ripple, known for XRP, has struck a deal to purchase Hidden Road, a prime brokerage firm, for $1.25 billion. This acquisition marks a significant step for Ripple in catering to institutional customers and broadening its services. CEO Brad Garlinghouse highlighted the necessity of enhancing infrastructure to meet the needs of large financial entities. The agreement involves a combination of cash, XRP tokens, and equity, with closure anticipated in the near future pending regulatory approval. Under Ripple, Hidden Road's founder, Marc Asch, will oversee the prime brokerage division. Prime brokers serve an essential role in facilitating...
news.headline: Ripple acquires Hidden Road for $1.25 billion, its biggest move yet to expand offerings for institutional clients.

news.summary: Despite the turmoil on Wall Street caused by tariffs, the U.S. labor market remains strong and resilient, defying concerns about economic weakness. In March, nonfarm payrolls saw a significant increase of 228,000, surpassing economists' expectations of 135,000 and the three-month average of 195,000. The previous month's employment growth was revised down to 117,000. Private sector payrolls spiked by 209,000, marking the highest increase since December 2024. Job growth was observed in several sectors including health care, social assistance, transportation and warehousing, and retail trade which saw gains due to the return of workers post-strike. Surprisingly, public sector payrolls also rose by 19,000. These numbers provide a positive outlook on the labor market despite concerns about the impact of tariffs on Wall Street.
news.headline: US labor market thrives as nonfarm payrolls spike by 228,000 in March, surpassing the 3-month average of 195,000 and beating economists' forecasts of 135,000. Private sector sees 209,000 new jobs, highest since December 2024, with employment spikes in health care, social assistance, transportation, warehousing, and retail trade. Public sector payrolls also rise by 19,000. Wall Street concerns over tariffs contrast with labor market strength. More details on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Wall Street received a warning as the probability of a U.S. recession has jumped to 60%, a significant increase from 40% last month, according to JPMorgan analyst Maia G. Crook. This rise is attributed to the recent tariff hikes, which are deemed as the "largest U.S. tax hike in nearly 60 years." The escalation of the tariff war with a fresh 22% increase has heightened fears of retaliation and potential economic slowdowns. Crook predicts that these tariffs will impact household and business spending, decrease sentiment, and disrupt global supply chains. The analyst also highlights the uncertainty surrounding trade policies and warns of potential ripple effects.
news.headline: JPMorgan raises U.S. recession risk to 60% due to 'largest tax hike in 60 years', as tariff war escalates and fear of economic slowdowns arise.

news.summary: In Alexandria, Virginia on April 8, 2025, the Military Officers Association of America (MOAA) reiterated its longstanding commitment to supporting a robust national defense, emphasizing the critical importance of the individuals who contribute to this effort. The MOAA's advocacy includes prioritizing the welfare of those who serve, highlighting the essential role played by their dedication and sacrifices. The organization stresses the necessity of adequate resources to uphold this defense, recognizing the significance of maintaining a strong military force.
news.headline: MOAA emphasizes not compromising troops/veterans for efficiency efforts in national defense.
Mexico's President Sheinbaum: Mexico's Economy Minister Ebrard to meet US Commerce Secretary Lutnick Tuesday.
US Trade Representative Greer: Reciprocal tariffs are generally lower in the Western hemisphere.
